content: You can't set up host domains in Thunderbird yet. It will be possible when Thunderbird Pro arrives.
creator: siffemoz

content:
Thunderbird uses mbox files, not EML file so I guess you should probably import your EML files with the importexport addon. https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-US/thunderbird/addon/importexporttools-ng/?src=ss

Thunderbird can export an EML file and if it is double clicked on can open and display the contents, but natively it does not use to format.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
BigAlBat said
I (reluctantly) migrated my xfinity email account to yahoo and in the process of getting Thunderbird to access the yahoo account, it wiped out ALL of my folders. The folders are still there and accessible in yahoo but no longer show up in Thunderbird. Is there an easy way to fix this and get Thunderbird to show the folders? Thanks.

Later: I was able to solve this problem by deleting my account in Thunderbird and creating a new account(which is identical to the one I deleted) and now the folders show up. BUT I should not have had to do this.
creator: BigAlBat

content:
That's how I would have done it anyway, rather than try to rejigger the existing Comcast account.

Also, as it turns out, despite what was initially presented last year by Comcast, the migration to Yahoo is voluntary.
creator: rednoise

content: Are those message there when you log into the GMX web interface? If not, they are gone. You could ask GMX to restore them.

content:
Try to start Windows 10 in safe mode with networking enabled.

Does the problem go away?

Windows safe mode disables anti-virus software. This is a troubleshooting step, not a solution.
creator: christ1
